lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: Hey! What can I do for ya' today?

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: I'm Rhoddwr, a simple forest dweller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's nice to meet you Rhoddwr, I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: And it's great to meet you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: I'm the leader of this small camp. We live amongst the trees, at one with the forest.

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: That's because it is! This is where we belong, this is where we began.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A nice sentiment, but we can't all live off the land.

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: Very true! As we expand and grow, nature refuses to adjust to our ways. The only other option is to destroy it to make room for growth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hopefully nature understands the necessity of it all.

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: History shows again and again that nature points out the folly of man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is why we should learn from the past and avoid making such mistakes.

lines.speaker: Rhoddwr Tan
lines.text: Indeed, although some mistakes have lasting effects that cannot be undone. Is there anything else you wanted to know?
